Caption: (Left) Previously reported connection of decoupled cell and (right) proposed connection of the same cell, as used in this work. This internal configuration corresponds to that of Fig. 2 (1).

Schematic representation of the decoupled blend cell cofigurations. The dark blue boxes represent the working electrodes, the grey box the lithium metal and the grey mesh represents the electrolyte and separator. Configuration 1 is assembled in a three electrode Swagelok cell. Configuration 2 is assembled in a two electrode cell with modified plunger and configuration 3 is based in two coin cells connected in parallel. (For interpretation of the references to color in this figure legend, the reader is referred to the web version of this article.)

a) (Top) Voltage profile (black line) and charge distribution (shaded areas) in a LiMn2O4: LiNi0.5Mn0.3Co0.2O2 50:50 blend obtained using the decoupled cell setup. The dashed black line shows the total (dis)charging current of the cell, set to achieve a cell nominal rate of C/10 (0.1C). (bottom) The C-rate calculated for each of the two materials with explicit values corresponding to maximum observed C-rate. C-rates are calculated from the current flowing through each electrode component, which is divided by its capacity. The dashed line is the overall nominal cell current divided by the sum of the capacities of both positive electrodes (set at C/10). b) Results of decoupled blend tests for compositions with different mass ratios (25 % LMO-75 % NMC (top) and 75 % LMO – 25 % NMC (bottom)) at C/5 (0.2C). Data taken from [38,39].
